title: To His Honoured Friend, Sir Thomas Heale.
content:
Stand by the magic of my powerful rhymes
    'Gainst all the indignation of the times.
    Age shall not wrong thee; or one jot abate
    Of thy both great and everlasting fate.
    While others perish, here's thy life decreed,
    Because begot of my immortal seed.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Robert Herrick delivers a powerful performance in "To His Honoured Friend, Sir Thomas Heale."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
